Title: GROWTH REGULATING FACTOR 7-mediated arbutin metabolism enhances rice salt tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to hydroquinone to form arbutin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances rice salt tolerance, likely by reducing ROS accumulation and improving antioxidant enzyme activities; also associated with increased grain size
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Salt-inducible; upregulated in OsGRF7 overexpression lines and downregulated in OsGRF7 RNAi/knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NA; untargeted metabolomics: NA; targeted metabolite profiling: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in GRF7-OE background; RT-qPCR; EMSA; transient transactivation assay; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay by HPLC-MS; targeted metabolite profiling; salt survival assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes arbutin accumulation, salt tolerance, and grain size
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsUGT1 expression is induced by salt and activated directly by OsGRF7. 2. Recombinant OsUGT1 glycosylates hydroquinone to produce arbutin in vitro. 3. OsUGT1 knockout reduces arbutin content, increases hydroquinone, lowers salt tolerance, and decreases grain size in the GRF7-OE background.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase arbutin for salt-tolerant and larger-grain rice; can be stacked with OsGRF7 activation strategies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes reduced grain size and lower salt tolerance; no direct penalty of enhanced activity separately established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from candidate rice UGTs as a salt-induced and OsGRF7-responsive arbutin biosynthesis gene

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to hydroquinone to form arbutin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances rice salt tolerance, likely by reducing ROS accumulation and improving antioxidant enzyme activities; also associated with increased grain size
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Salt-inducible; upregulated in OsGRF7 overexpression lines and downregulated in OsGRF7 RNAi/knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NA; untargeted metabolomics: NA; targeted metabolite profiling: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in GRF7-OE background; RT-qPCR; EMSA; transient transactivation assay; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay by HPLC-MS; targeted metabolite profiling; salt survival assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes arbutin accumulation, salt tolerance, and grain size
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsUGT5 expression is induced by salt and activated directly by OsGRF7. 2. Recombinant OsUGT5 glycosylates hydroquinone to produce arbutin in vitro. 3. OsUGT5 knockout reduces arbutin content, increases hydroquinone, lowers salt tolerance, and decreases grain size in the GRF7-OE background.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase arbutin for salt-tolerant and larger-grain rice; can be stacked with OsGRF7 activation strategies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes reduced grain size and lower salt tolerance; no direct penalty of enhanced activity separately established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified from candidate rice UGTs as a salt-induced and OsGRF7-responsive arbutin biosynthesis gene

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances rice salt tolerance, likely by promoting ROS scavenging; also associated with grain size improvement
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsUGT1 and OsUGT5 expression by directly binding their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Salt stress; protein stability control by OsFBO13-mediated ubiquitination and degradation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with OsGIF coactivators; targeted by SCF^OsFBO13 E3 ligase complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct positive regulator of arbutin biosynthetic genes and major positive controller of arbutin accumulation under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Studied in rice seedlings; functional under normal and salt stress conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seedlings under salt stress; transgenic overexpression elevates target gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM or 140 mM NaCl; exogenous arbutin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NA; untargeted metabolomics: NA; targeted metabolite profiling: NA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression, RNAi, knockout, transcriptomics, metabolomics, exogenous arbutin rescue, EMSA, transient transactivation assays, targeted metabolite profiling, salt survival assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes arbutin accumulation, salt tolerance, and grain size; reduces hydroquinone accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGRF7 overexpression increases salt tolerance and arbutin content, whereas RNAi/knockout causes salt sensitivity and reduced arbutin. 2. Exogenous arbutin rescues the salt-sensitive phenotype of OsGRF7-deficient plants. 3. OsGRF7 directly binds and activates OsUGT1 and OsUGT5 to stimulate arbutin biosynthesis. 4. OsGRF7 also positively affects grain size.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering rice with enhanced salt tolerance and larger grain via boosting arbutin metabolism; stabilization of OsGRF7 may be advantageous
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression shortens seedling length/negatively affects plant height under normal conditions based on prior and current observations
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lysine K365 is the primary ubiquitination site relevant to OsFBO13-mediated degradation

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box domain-containing protein with UBQ domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances salt tolerance and is associated with larger grain size
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Arbutin biosynthesis via control of regulator stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes degradation of OsGRF7, thereby decreasing OsUGT1 and OsUGT5 transcript levels indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Integral part of SCF^OsFBO13 E3 ligase complex with OSK proteins
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative post-translational regulator that limits arbutin accumulation by destabilizing the positive TF OsGRF7
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Studied in rice seedlings under normal growth conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression lines reduce arbutin and salt tolerance; knockout lines increase arbutin and salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid, BiFC, split luciferase complementation, coimmunoprecipitation, overexpression, CRISPR/Cas9 knockout, cell-free degradation assay, targeted metabolite profiling, salt survival assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates arbutin accumulation, salt tolerance, and grain size by degrading OsGRF7
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFBO13 physically interacts with OsGRF7 and forms an SCF-type E3 ligase complex with OSK proteins. 2. OsFBO13 promotes 26S proteasome-dependent degradation of OsGRF7. 3. OsFBO13 overexpression reduces arbutin content, salt tolerance, and grain size, whereas knockout has opposite effects. 4. OsUGT1 and OsUGT5 transcript levels decrease in OsFBO13 overexpression plants.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function editing of OsFBO13 could stabilize OsGRF7 and increase arbutin, salt tolerance, and grain size
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression decreases grain size and salt tolerance; knockout may alter broader ubiquitin signaling not explored
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsGRF7 K365 is a primary ubiquitination site for OsFBO13-mediated repression
